<!--
=============================================================================
DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE — it is the persistent template every release copies from.

This template is NEVER cleared. The per-release copy is what gets consumed and
removed; the permanent record of past releases lives in the root CHANGELOG.md.

Preparing a release:

  1. Copy this template to the dated per-release file (UTC):
       cp .github/release-note-template.md \
          release-notes/release-note-$(date -u +%Y%m%d-%H%M).md
  2. Read every file under `.changeset/*.md` (these are the pending changes that
     determine the next version and get consumed at release time) for context.
  3. In the new dated file (NOT this template), replace each `(write here)`
     block with a brief, user-facing summary. Delete an entire section if there
     is genuinely nothing for it.
  4. Open the PR into `develop` with the `release-automation` label. CI fills in
     the version bump and the CHANGELOG entry for you.

Style rules:

  - One bullet = one product-level fact a user / agent developer cares about.
    6–12 words. Plain prose.
  - Cluster purely-technical items (refactors, dep bumps, infra / CI, type
    fixes, internal renames) into a single trailing bullet per section:
      Fixed       → "Few technical bugs fixed"
      New Feature → "Technical enhancement"
      Changed     → "Technical enhancement"
    Don't expand the technical bucket into individual bullets.
  - No PR / issue refs, no version numbers, no author thanks, no "see #N" links.
  - English only. Tight.
  - If a section ends up with only the technical-bucket bullet, keep it. If a
    section has zero bullets, delete the whole section including its heading.

How the pipeline uses the dated file:

  - `.github/workflows/require-release-note.yml` gates `develop → main` PRs on a
    dated file being present with all three sections and no `(write here)`
    placeholder. The PR can't merge until the gate is green.
  - `.github/workflows/release.yml` strips this leading comment and uses the
    remaining prose as the GitHub Release body, and the same prose becomes the
    new `## vX.Y.Z` section in the root CHANGELOG.md.
  - After the release is tagged, the dated file is deleted by the automated
    cleanup PR back into `develop` (this template stays).
=============================================================================
-->

## Fixed

- (write here)

## New Feature

- (write here)

## Changed

- (write here)
